no-member.title: False positives when 'type' builtin is overwritten
no-member.note: Confirmed: when 'type' builtin is shadowed by a parameter, pylint still infers the call as returning the actual class, leading to false E1101. Real bug.

no-member.title: False positive for numpy 2.4.0+
no-member.note: Confirmed E1101 'Instance of finfo has no eps member' with numpy 2.4.4 installed. Reporter shows the bug surfaces with numpy 2.4.0 and disappears with numpy 2.3.5 — astroid's numpy brain doesn't track finfo's properties on numpy 2.4+. Oldest report; canonical for this bug.

no-member.title: False positive no-member
no-member.note: Confirmed E1101 false positive: '[sth.name for sth, _ in [lorem, ipsum, dolor]]' where each list element is a 2-tuple (Foo, int). pylint cannot infer the tuple-unpacking type from list of homogeneous tuples.

no-member.title: Invalid no-name-in-module when shadowing a base module with an alias then calling a method named format on that alias
no-member.note: Confirmed E0611 'No name utils in module my_module.utils' triggered by combination of 'import my_module.utils as my_module' AND calling method named 'format' on the alias. Specific to .format() method name shadowing.

no-member.title: Instance of 'DatetimeIndex' has no 'to_pydatetime' member Pylint (E1101:no-member)
no-member.note: Confirmed E1101 'Instance of DatetimeIndex has no to_pydatetime member' on pandas 3.0.3. Oldest report (2025-01) of the broader 'pandas DatetimeIndex brain incomplete' family of false positives. Canonical for this bug cluster — #10796 reports the same root cause for a different missing member ('date').

no-member.title: `no-member` when creating an object through an inherited factory method
no-member.note: Confirmed E1101: Child.from_dict() through inherited factory loses subclass attributes. astroid factory-classmethod inference gap.

no-member.title: Incorrect type inferred when inner class definition closes over variable from outer class’s method
no-member.note: Confirmed E1101: closure over 'this = self' in nested class loses Outer type info; 'x.say_hello()' flagged as missing member.

no-member.title: `no-member` error when accessing a sub class variable with `self` returned from a super class method
no-member.note: Confirmed E1101: super().save() return narrowed to Foo, loses Bar.value attribute.

no-member.title: time.sleep false negative!
no-member.note: Confirmed false negative: 'from time import time; time.sleep(1)' (calling .sleep on the time *function*) is not flagged no-member even though time.time is a function.

no-member.title: False-negative: missing 'no-member' in case of private member function invoke without mangling
no-member.note: Confirmed false negative: 'Foo.__foo' name-mangling access from outside the class is not flagged no-member (the issue is FN — should suggest _Foo__foo).

no-member.title: E1507 (invalid-envvar-value) does not accept `builtins.str` as a valid type for `os.getenv` argument
no-member.note: Confirmed E1507 false positive: os.getenv(self.name) where self.name is annotated as str — invalid-envvar-value fires.

no-member.title: Coroutine type inferred incorrectly
no-member.note: Confirmed E1101 false positive: 'g = f(); g.send(None)' where f is async — coroutine type not modeled, g inferred as bytes.

no-member.title: False positive no-member when accessing a mangled instance variable of a super class after calling the constructor of the super class
no-member.note: Confirmed E1101: 'TestSub()._Test__test' (private name-mangling access from outside) flagged no-member even though attribute exists at runtime.

no-member.title: False positive no-member with sys.stdin.buffer.peek()
no-member.note: Confirmed E1101: 'sys.stdin.buffer.peek(16)' flagged no-member. astroid wrongly types stdin.buffer as BufferedWriter instead of BufferedReader.

no-member.title: no-member false positive from NewType
no-member.note: Confirmed E1101: NewType wrapping a class loses member access ('b.value' on B=NewType('B', A) flagged no-member).

dataclasses.title: Unexpected kwarg false-positive when using TypeVarTuple
dataclasses.note: Verified on pylint 4.1.0-dev0 / astroid 4.2.0b3 (origin/main): E1123 'Unexpected keyword argument value' fires on Upper[str, int](value="hello") where Upper inherits from Middle[T, *Shape] which inherits from a kw_only=True dataclass Base. Previous UNCLEAR was because pylint 4.0.5 + astroid 4.0.2 lacks PEP 695 TypeVarTuple support; with newer astroid the bug surfaces. Reporter's hypothesis (MCharming98 comment): astroid's _infer_sequence_helper fails when it hits a Starred node containing TypeVarTuple, which silently breaks MRO inference in brain_dataclasses. Related to #10972.

dataclasses.title: Second order inheritance of dataclasses with default values/`init=False` causes `E1120 no-value-for-parameter`
dataclasses.note: Confirmed E1120 'no-value-for-parameter' on @dataclass three-level inheritance where intermediate level has 'field(default=..., init=False)'. astroid dataclass init signature reconstruction.

dataclasses.title: False positive `no-member` when wrapping dataclasses `field`
dataclasses.note: Confirmed false positive E1101: wrapping dataclasses.field() in a helper that returns it (return field(**kw)) makes pylint see attribute as Field instead of the annotated type.

dataclasses.title: Unexpected keyword argument for Generic dataclass with ABC bounded TypeVar
dataclasses.note: Confirmed E1123 unexpected-keyword-arg: @dataclass with Generic[_T] where _T has 'bound=Base|AbstractBase' (Union bound). pylint loses 'field' kwarg through the bound resolution.

dataclasses.title: False-positive E1121 when using dataclass with init=False
dataclasses.note: Confirmed E1121 false positive: dataclass A with init=False inherited by B(A) — B('a','b') flagged 'too many positional args' even though it is valid.

dataclasses.title: False positive: Class has no '__dataclass_fields__' member (no-member)
dataclasses.note: Confirmed E1101: '__dataclass_fields__' attribute on a @dataclass is not modeled by astroid dataclass brain.

dataclasses.title: pylint doesn't know re.Pattern members
dataclasses.note: Confirmed E1101: dataclass with field 're.Pattern[str]' — accessing C.pattern_instance.pattern reports no-member. astroid re brain doesn't propagate through dataclass.

dataclasses.title: How to fix pylint E1134: Non-mapping value X is used in a mapping context (not-a-mapping)
dataclasses.note: Confirmed E1134 false positive: dataclass with __post_init__ doing 'self.f_four = One(**self.f_four)' where f_four is dict before being replaced by One instance — pylint treats f_four as One type from the annotation.

typing.title: Abstract method false-positive when using TypeVarTuple
typing.note: Confirmed: E0110 false positive on Concrete that satisfies abstract Base.get_item via TypeVarTuple chain (PEP 695). pylint cannot follow *Shape forwarding through inheritance.

typing.title: pylint fails to resolve typing.Self from base classes
typing.note: Confirmed: typing.Self from a base-class method called via super() does not narrow to the subclass type. False E1101 on B().meth().f2().

typing.title: Type narrowing fails due to unrelated instance assignment
typing.note: Confirmed: type narrowing via isinstance(...) and isinstance(stmt.src, ...) within a single 'and' breaks when an unrelated function in the same module constructs another concrete subclass. Removing that function eliminates the false positive.

typing.title: False positive E1136: unsubscriptable-object when generic class defines __class_getitem__
typing.note: Confirmed E1136: when a generic class defines __class_getitem__ AND __getitem__, instance of Test[int]([...]) is still flagged as unsubscriptable.

typing.title: False positive with `arguments-differ` rule in overridden overloaded methods in subclass
typing.note: Confirmed W0221 arguments-differ false positive on overloaded @staticmethod overrides — pylint compares only the first @overload signature of the base class, not the full overload set.

typing.title: False positive on __required__ and __optional__ on TypedDict.
typing.note: Confirmed E1101: TypedDict.__required_keys__ / __optional_keys__ flagged as unknown members. Should be supported by astroid TypedDict brain.

typing.title: Class 'str' has no '__value__' member on PEP 695 TypeAliasType
typing.note: Confirmed E1101 'Class str has no __value__ member' on 'type X = str; print(X.__value__)' (testing_pylintrc). Maintainer-blessed canonical: Jacob Walls redirected this ticket via comment to focus on the __value__ no-member issue (the original body covered 3 PEP-695 bugs but the other two are tracked elsewhere — #9335 closed for the undefined-variable T case, #9884 for redefined-outer-name). Has 'Needs PR' label.

typing.title: False positive on not-callable when inheriting from `typing.IO`
typing.note: Confirmed E1102 not-callable on subclass-of-typing.IO[T] when instantiated as Z[str]() while X[str]()/Y[str]() with plain typing.Generic work. typing.IO ClassVar inference issue.

typing.title: False-positive E1101 (no-member) inheriting from `set[int]`
typing.note: Confirmed E1101: subclass of 'set[int]' loses inferred attributes. astroid generic-builtin-subclass inheritance gap.

typing.title: False positive E1126 (invalid-sequence-index) on generic type alias with forward ref
typing.note: Confirmed E1126 false positive: 'Alias2: TypeAlias = "list[T]"' (string form) triggers invalid-sequence-index, while non-string form 'list[T]' does not.

typing.title: `redefined-outer-name` (`W0621`) - false positive on 3.12 type aliases
typing.note: Confirmed W0621 redefined-outer-name on 'type Foo[T] = T; def foo[T]: ...' (PEP 695 type-params should be statement-scoped, not module-scoped). Oldest report; has 'Needs PR' label. Canonical for this bug cluster.

typing.title: False positive for `unnecessary-ellipsis` on `Protocol` methods
typing.note: Confirmed W2301 unnecessary-ellipsis false positive on Protocol method body. The '...' is required for type-checkers like pyright to infer non-implementation.

typing.title: False positive `duplicate-bases` with `TypedDict`
typing.note: Confirmed E0241 duplicate-bases false positive on 'class ABCD(ABC):' where ABC inherits multiple TypedDicts qualified via 'typing.TypedDict'. Works fine with unqualified TypedDict import.

typing.title: pylint does not support typing.Self when override
typing.note: Confirmed E1101 false positive: super(B, cls).f1() with @classmethod and typing.Self return type doesn't narrow back to B. Mirror of #10807.

typing.title: False-positive W0236 (and W0221) when implementing an optional protocol method
typing.note: Confirmed: W0221 + W0236 false positives when Class(Proto) implements a Proto @property as a regular method. Real overload-vs-property mismatch.

typing.title: protected-access false positive with Generic classes
typing.note: RE-VERIFIED: protected-access W0212 STILL fires on 'Parent._foo(self)' in Child._foo override when Parent is Generic[T]. Initial FIXED determination was wrong because pylint's own pylintrc disables protected-access.

typing.title: invalid-name check for TypeVar should allow for digits in names
typing.note: RE-VERIFIED: C0103 STILL fires on 'Ec2T = TypeVar("Ec2T")' under default typevar-rgx. Initial FIXED determination was wrong because pylint's own pylintrc disables invalid-name.

typing.title: False positive `no-member` for `TypeAlias` `__origin__`
typing.note: Confirmed E1101: dict alias DictAlias = dict[int, float] has __origin__ at runtime but pylint reports 'Class dict has no __origin__ member'.

typing.title: Incorrect type inferance after a conditional raise
typing.note: Confirmed E1130: type narrowing after 'if not isinstance(operand, (int, float, complex)): raise' is not applied to subsequent return -operand.

typing.title: False negative for `isinstance-second-argument-not-valid-type` (parameterized generic)
typing.note: Confirmed false negative: isinstance-second-argument-not-valid-type is NOT raised on 'isinstance(0, list[int])' even though parameterized generics raise TypeError at runtime.

typing.title: Generic type of setter is leaking across instances
typing.note: Confirmed E1130 false positive: Generic[T] setter type narrowing 'leaks' across instances — pylint applies the last setter's T to a different instance with different actual generic type.

typing.title: Incorrect unsupported-membership-test error on TypedDict
typing.note: Confirmed E1135 unsupported-membership-test false positive: 'while x is None or "a" in x:' on Optional[TypedDict] — pylint doesn't narrow after 'or'.

typing.title: Missing Class Docstring false positive when inheriting from a generic class of a TypedDict instance
typing.note: RE-VERIFIED: C0116 missing-function-docstring STILL fires on Child(Bases[TypedDict-functional]).method (only Child class has docstring, override method doesn't). Initial determination was confused by the pylintrc.

typing.title: unused-import false positive for names in string argument to typing.cast
typing.note: Confirmed W0611 false positive: 'paths = t.cast("set[Path]", set())' flags Path as unused even though it's in the cast type string.

typing.title: False positive no-member on member of generic parent class with overridden __class_getitem__
typing.note: Already verified REPRO in session 4.

typing.title: False positive invalid-overridden-method for async generators overriding AsyncIterable
typing.note: Confirmed W0236 false positive: async generator (uses yield) overriding a Protocol method returning AsyncIterable[str] flagged invalid-overridden-method. pylint should detect 'yield' to recognize async-generator vs coroutine.

typing.title: False positive: Tuple claimed unsubscriptable when using NewType
typing.note: Confirmed E1136: NewType('T', tuple[float,float]) — instance treated as unsubscriptable. astroid doesn't model NewType-wrapped tuples.

typing.title: False positive no-member for NamedTuple._replace()
typing.note: Confirmed E1101: 'self._replace(...)' on NamedTuple subclass not recognized. astroid NamedTuple brain doesn't expose _replace via subclass.

typing.title: Classvar[Optional[Tuple]] must be unpacked
typing.note: Confirmed E0633: 'a, b = cls.cached_a_and_b()' where return is ClassVar[Optional[Tuple[A,B]]] still raises unpacking-non-sequence.

typing.title: Pylint gets confused by functools.singledispatch and assumes wrong return types of its registered functions.
typing.note: Confirmed E1101: @singledispatch-registered overload returning HasAttributeX not picked up — pylint still infers result as HasNoAttributeX from generic fallback.

typing.title: False positive not-an-iterable for typing.NewType
typing.note: Confirmed E1133: 'a = NewType("a", List[int])'; 'for x in fun()' where fun returns a — still flagged not-an-iterable.

imports.title: Pylint skipping similarly named project directory.
imports.note: Confirmed bug; same-prefix sibling directories get incorrectly skipped by _discover_files() because skip_subtrees uses string prefix match. Author has WIP fix.

imports.title: pylint only catching `cyclic-import` in parallel mode
imports.note: Confirmed by reporter: cyclic-import only fires under -j0 or -j>=2, not -j1. Internal: serial codepath misses the cycle detector.

imports.title: `ungrouped-imports` / `wrong-import-order` : FP because only two isort's options are taken into account
imports.note: Confirmed ungrouped-imports/wrong-import-order false positive: pylint only consults isort's 'known_first_party'/'known_third_party' sections, ignoring custom sections (known_platforms, known_frameworks, …).

imports.title: False positive `import-self` when attempting relative import of misspelled submodule name in a package.
imports.note: Confirmed W0406 import-self false positive: 'from . import misspelled_module_name' on a missing submodule incorrectly reports 'Module import itself' instead of import-error.

imports.title: Linting fails if module contains module of the same name
imports.note: Confirmed F0010/parse-error: 'pylint a/' where a/ contains a.py (same name as dir) without __init__.py fails with 'Unable to load file a/__init__.py'.

enum.title: pylint does not recognise `from ... import` inside an `IntEnum` class body as producing enum members. It infers the members as `int` instead of `LogLevel`, causing a spurious `E1101: Instance of 'int' has no 'value' member (no-member)` error.
enum.note: Confirmed: E1101 false positive — pylint/astroid does not treat 'from x import y as Z' inside an IntEnum body as producing enum members; infers them as int.

enum.title: `invalid-envvar-value` does not support StrEnum
enum.note: Confirmed: E1507 (invalid-envvar-value) triggers for StrEnum member used as os.getenv arg, even though StrEnum members ARE strings. Needs StrEnum support in the envvar checker.

enum.title: False positive E1121: Too many positional arguments error for Subclassed Enums instanciated with functional API
enum.note: Confirmed E1121 false positive: CustomEnum('MyEnum', {'RED': 1}) (subclass of Enum used via Enum functional API) triggers too-many-function-args, while base Enum() does not. astroid does not propagate the functional __call__ signature through subclassing.

enum.title: False positive `invalid-overridden-method` when overridding `Enum.value`
enum.note: Confirmed W0236 false positive: overriding enum.Enum.value (a DynamicClassAttribute) with @property triggers invalid-overridden-method.

enum.title: E0238 false positive when defining __slots__ in IntEnum class
enum.note: Confirmed E0238 invalid-slots false positive: '__slots__ = ()' inside an IntEnum body is flagged invalid although it is valid (empty tuple is acceptable __slots__).

enum.title: `isinstance-second-argument-not-valid-type` does not handle `Enum.enum`
enum.note: Confirmed W1116 false positive: Enum functional form 'Direction = Enum("Direction", [...])' not recognized as a valid isinstance second arg.

decorators.title: pylint does not honor decorator return type when using @ syntax
decorators.note: Confirmed: E1101 false positive — pylint ignores decorator's annotated return type when applied via '@' syntax (works with explicit f = decorator(f_) form). Marked Duplicate label upstream.

decorators.title: Changing the number of function arguments when using a decorator does not work.
decorators.note: Confirmed via reporter: decorator with Concatenate[…, P] adds new positional arg but pylint doesn't pick up the new signature; E1121 too-many-function-args false positive.

decorators.title: False positive: `no-member` when inner function uses the `@classmethod` decorator
decorators.note: Confirmed E1101 false positive: @classmethod-decorated inner function inside def test() treated as member of 'test' function.

decorators.title: False positive for ``unexpected-keyword-arg`` for decorators with inner inner function
decorators.note: Confirmed E1123 false positive: 'example_func(6, print_me="hello")' decorated with example_decorator (top-level wrapper) — pylint still flags print_me as unexpected kwarg.

decorators.title: False positive: unsubscriptable-object when using classmethod and property together
decorators.note: Confirmed E1136 false positive: @classmethod @property combination for 'all_constants' returning List[int] flagged unsubscriptable.

decorators.title: Attributes added by a decorator are raised as missing members errors
decorators.note: Confirmed E1101: @functools.wraps decorator returning a NamedTuple-wrapped instance loses inferred type — 'mod.init' and 'mod.apply' flagged no-member.

decorators.title: false positive: E1111 assignment-from-no-return when using function name "lazy"
decorators.note: Confirmed E1111 false positive: function named 'lazy' as decorator triggers assignment-from-no-return; renaming the function (e.g. 'lazy2') fixes it. astroid name-based heuristic confuses 'lazy' specifically.

decorators.title: Type changes in decorators are not detected
decorators.note: Confirmed E1101: @cast_to_str decorator returning Callable[..., str] doesn't change inferred return type — get_num().strip() still flagged.

control-flow.title: possibly-used-before-assignment false negative when variable has type annotation
control-flow.note: Confirmed false negative: when 'err' has a forward type annotation but only is assigned in some except clauses, pylint does not flag possibly-used-before-assignment at the later use site. Removing the annotation makes pylint flag it.

control-flow.title: Property union type inference issue: E1102 not-callable false positive
control-flow.note: Confirmed E1102 false positive: property returning CallableType|None, even after 'if self.processor:' narrowing, still triggers not-callable. Direct attribute access works.

control-flow.title: False positive `ungrouped-imports` with if os.name
control-flow.note: Confirmed C0412 ungrouped-imports false positive across if/else branches (cross-platform import pattern). Already linked to #7735, #3382 by reporter.

control-flow.title: False positive `used-before-assignment`
control-flow.note: Confirmed E0601 used-before-assignment on 'v' — control-flow analysis treats the early 'if x not in ["A"]:' as not narrowing later 'elif x == "A":'. Reporter says it should at minimum be downgraded to possibly-used-before-assignment.

control-flow.title: W0640 cell-var-from-loop false positive in Generator Comprehension of functions
control-flow.note: Confirmed W0640 cell-var-from-loop FP on '(lambda: print(i) for i in range(10))' generator expression. The lambda *is* the generator's element so each i binding is correct.

control-flow.title: [deprecated-x] Message trigger even in old interpreter compatibility code
control-flow.note: Confirmed W4904 deprecated-class triggered inside 'if sys.version_info >= (3, 12): ... else: ...' compatibility blocks. pylint should not warn the older branch when guarded.

control-flow.title: False positive E0601: used-before-assignment in try and while block
control-flow.note: Confirmed E0601 used-before-assignment false positive: 'nonlocal a' inside a try/except or while block fails to mark 'a' as resolvable; works fine for for/range loops.

control-flow.title: `used-before-assignment` for assignment in inner try when outer try returns
control-flow.note: Confirmed E0601 used-before-assignment: nested 'try: results=[]' inside except branch is not seen as assigning before the final 'return results'.

control-flow.title: False negative for NameError from calling inner function before variable is assigned
control-flow.note: Confirmed false negative: inner function references 'i' that is only later assigned by the for-loop; the first inner() call would raise NameError at runtime but pylint stays silent.

control-flow.title: W0631: Using possibly undefined loop variable (undefined-loop-variable)
control-flow.note: Confirmed W0631 undefined-loop-variable: 'return number' after 'if not numbers: return None' guard + for loop is still flagged. Pylint can't see the guard ensures non-empty iter.

control-flow.title: cell-var-from-loop ignores generator expressions
control-flow.note: Confirmed false negative W0640 cell-var-from-loop on generator-expression captured from outer loop variable: '(value for value in [1,2] if value == loop_var)' inside 'for loop_var in ...:' is not flagged.

control-flow.title: Spurious 'undefined-variable' warning with postponed annotations enabled
control-flow.note: Confirmed E0602 undefined-variable false positive: 'from __future__ import annotations' + class B nested in class A used as annotation in sibling class C.

control-flow.title: `undefined-variable` false positive when method returns instance type when the class is inside a function
control-flow.note: Confirmed E0602 false positive: 'def foo(self) -> Foo:' inside a function-nested class with 'from __future__ import annotations' raises undefined-variable on the return type.

control-flow.title: undefined-variable regression since 2.6.0
control-flow.note: Confirmed E0602 undefined-variable false positive: '@CONVERTER.register()' as decorator for a class defined inside another class still fails.

control-flow.title: False positive for repeated-keywords when dict item is popped off
control-flow.note: Confirmed E1132 false positive: 'foo(c=x, **d)' after 'd.pop("c")' still flags repeated-keyword. pylint can't track dict mutation.

control-flow.title: False positive `used-before-assignment` with multi-item `with` statement and tuple target
control-flow.note: Confirmed E0601 false positive: 'with f() as (a, b), g(a) as c:' — tuple-target multi-item with statement — pylint sees 'a' as undefined when used in g(a).

control-flow.title: False negative: expected undefined-variable when deleted variable used
control-flow.note: Confirmed FN persists: 'del abc; print(abc)' inside function is not flagged undefined-variable (10/10) even though Python raises UnboundLocalError at runtime.

control-flow.title: False positive for unexpected-keyword-arg using `**` operator
control-flow.note: Confirmed E1123 false positive: 'd["arg"] = d["b"]; del d["b"]; func(**d)' still flags 'b' as unexpected kwarg even though it was deleted.

control-flow.title: E1101 false-positive/-negtive when mutating typess of dictionary values
control-flow.note: Confirmed mixed E1101 FP/FN: mutating dict values via 'for k,v in d.items(): d[k] = transform(v)' — pylint still sees old type. Real but the FN side (case 3) is the trickier part.

control-flow.title: `used-before-assignment` false positive on multiple-target assignment
control-flow.note: Confirmed E0601 false positive: 'a = b[id(a)] = 0' multiple-target assignment with target-n using target-n+1 reports 'a' used before assignment.

control-flow.title: False `unbalanced-tuple-unpacking` report
control-flow.note: Confirmed W0632 false positive: 'a, b = f("12")' where f appends to a list — pylint can't track that the list ultimately has 2 elements at unpack time.

control-flow.title: Negation and None
control-flow.note: Confirmed E1130 false positive: property with 'if self.__val is None: self.__val = 42; return self.__val' — pylint doesn't narrow __val to int after assignment, flags unary minus.

control-flow.title: False positive 'not-an-iterable' when using deferred initialization in a property
control-flow.note: Confirmed E1133 false positive: 'getattr(obj, "names")' where names is a property doing deferred init returning a list. pylint can't see through getattr+property+deferred-init.

control-flow.title: False positive used-before-assignment for return type
control-flow.note: Confirmed E0601 false positive: 'def foo(self) -> bool:' inside class with later 'def bool(self) -> bool' method — return annotation 'bool' confused with sibling method.

control-flow.title: False positive method-hidden in overridden methods
control-flow.note: Confirmed E0202 method-hidden false positive: Extended.func defined while Base.__init__ sets self.func=func — but Extended overrides __init__ to NOT call super, so no actual conflict.

control-flow.title: False negative ``undefined-variable`` for decorators in multiple scenario
control-flow.note: Confirmed but with different message: '@x.getter' in subclass B(A) where x is property on A — pylint flags W0236 invalid-overridden-method instead of expected undefined-variable. Either way the bug surfaces.

control-flow.title: false positive: unsupported-membership-test
control-flow.note: Confirmed E1135 unsupported-membership-test false positive: 'cls.all()' return value, where all() returns a list assigned to cls._all, treated as non-membership-test-supporting.

control-flow.title: Strange Emit of E0601 where something about shadowing a global would be clearer
control-flow.note: Confirmed E0601: '@property def type(self):' shadowing builtin 'type' in same class causes E0601 used-before-assignment on annotation 'thing_type: type' in __init__. Should report shadowing instead.

control-flow.title: Invalid report about "Possible unbalanced tuple unpacking"
control-flow.note: Confirmed W0632 unbalanced-tuple-unpacking false positive: chained 'return _fn_b() + (3,)' where _fn_b returns a 2-tuple — pylint sees right-side as 4 values.

control-flow.title: Unstable result of unsubscriptable-object error within if/elif/else block
control-flow.note: Confirmed E1136: 'a = a[1:]' inside elif still triggers unsubscriptable-object when initial 'a = None' is in outer scope. Control-flow narrowing miss.

control-flow.title: false redefined-outer-name when using `del`
control-flow.note: Confirmed W0621: 'def foo(): x = 2; del x; x = 3' inside function where outer scope has x=1 — redefined-outer-name still raised. Reporter argues del breaks the shadowing.

crash.title: Improper handling of log message format bytestrings
crash.note: Confirmed: crash (F0002 / UnicodeDecodeError) in pylint.checkers.logging._check_format_string when format string is a non-UTF8 bytes object. Direct fix in logging.py line 333 (use .decode(errors='replace') or str()).

crash.title: Crash with AstroidBuildingError when inheriting from a generic dataclass that has __init_subclass__
crash.note: Crash confirmed: AstroidBuildingError when a dataclass inherits unparameterized from a generic dataclass that defines __init_subclass__. Trace points to astroid brain_dataclasses._find_arguments_from_base_classes. Astroid bug.

crash.title: Crash in ``consider-using-enumerate`` when using ``len(range())`` and using the index to access an element of a list inside and element of a list
crash.note: Crash in consider-using-enumerate when 'len(range(...))' used with nested list indexing. Already labeled 'Needs PR'.

crash.title: Crash ``Building error when trying to create ast representation of module 'multiprocessing.process'``
crash.note: Confirmed crash pattern: project has its own 'multiprocessing/' directory shadowing stdlib multiprocessing → astroid brain_multiprocessing transform raises TypeError. Astroid brain bug.

crash.title: Recursive defining a function with classmethod decorator triggers crashing
crash.note: Confirmed crash: recursive 'super(C, cls).foo(a)' on @classmethod outside any class triggers AstroidError → F0002 fatal.

crash.title: Defining collections.namedtuple object trigger an AstroidError
crash.note: Confirmed crash: 'namedtuple("mu", ["µ"])' (Unicode identifier name) triggers AstroidError in brain_namedtuple_enum.infer_named_tuple.

crash.title: Unfound astroid.exceptions.InferenceError causes a crash
crash.note: Confirmed crash: 'C.p.fset' where p is a getter-only @property raises InferenceError → F0002 fatal.

crash.title: Pylint is crashing with astroid.exceptions.StatementMissing: Statement not found on <Module.builtins l.0
crash.note: Reproduced. Self-contained minimal repro: a class with both a method named after a site builtin (license/copyright/credits/exit/quit) AND that name also bound as an instance attribute via self.NAME = ... in __init__. This path enters class_checker.visit_functiondef's ancestor.lookup(node.name) branch which raises StatementMissing on Module.builtins. Confirmed crash on pylint 3.3.8/astroid 3.3.11, pylint 4.0.5/astroid 4.0.2 (THIS REPO), pylint 4.0.5/astroid 4.0.4, AND pylint 4.1.0-dev0/astroid 4.2.0b3 (latest main). Previous FIXED determination was incorrect — it was based on the simpler sub-case 'tuple = namedtuple(None, [])' which IS fixed in astroid 4.0.0, but that's a different code path (safe_infer → pytype → ancestors) than the funkwhale-flavored one (class_checker → ancestor.lookup → _filter_stmts → statement). The deeper bug is still live.

crash.title: Maximum recursion depth crash with pyreverse ``-S`` option
crash.note: Verified on pylint 4.0.5: pyreverse -S (recursive associations) on Pierre's 2022 minimal snippet (numpy.zeros class attribute) does not crash but hangs >60s without producing output. The original max-recursion crash from 2020 was fixed but the perf regression that Pierre flagged in 2022 ('not crashing but taking really fucking long') is still present. Workaround is to use bounded -s2 instead of -S; -s2 finishes in seconds and produces classes.dot.

crash.title: Pyreverse: ValueError: need more than 1 value to unpack
crash.note: Verified on pylint 4.0.5: pyreverse -c <unqualified-name> <package> still crashes, but with a different exception than the 2013 ValueError. Now: AttributeError: 'EmptyNode' object has no attribute 'name' in pyreverse/inspector.py:467. Same UX problem (poor error message for bad input). DudeNr33's 2023 analysis on the issue says the workaround is to use fully-qualified name (pyreverse -c top.mod.Class pkg). This is largely a UX/error-reporting issue, not a 'classes are wrong' bug.

naming-shadowing.title: Started getting "invalid name" errors on a module-level global variable after upgrade to pylint 4.0.4
naming-shadowing.note: RE-VERIFIED: pylint's local pylintrc disables invalid-name which masked the bug in initial test. With --rcfile=testing_pylintrc the C0103 invalid-name STILL fires on '_g_is_installed = False; def x(): global _g_is_installed; _g_is_installed = True'. The fix from c66868215 (#3585) only treats reassignment as variable when there are MULTIPLE module-level assignments to the same name. The global-statement reassignment from within a function doesn't count as a separate module-level assignment by frame().getattr().

naming-shadowing.title: False positive for constant naming style inside __main__ block
naming-shadowing.note: RE-VERIFIED: still fires with --rcfile=testing_pylintrc. 'exit_code = main()' inside 'if __name__ == "__main__":' block still triggers C0103. Single assignment treated as constant.

naming-shadowing.title: False negatives for `disallowed-name` due to control flow in NameChecker
naming-shadowing.note: Genuine internal bug per maintainer note: NameChecker short-circuits skip 'disallowed-name' along with 'invalid-name' in several control-flow paths. Code reference provided.

naming-shadowing.title: redefined-outer-name not emitted for names in enclosing namespace
naming-shadowing.note: Confirmed FN: 'def wrapper(): x=42; def f(x):' — pylint doesn't report redefined-outer-name when outer is enclosing fn scope, only when outer is module-level.

naming-shadowing.title: Incorrect report "unused-import" when assigning to class attribute with the same name as the imported module.
naming-shadowing.note: Confirmed W0611 false positive: 'import gettext' used in 'translation: gettext.NullTranslations' annotation but shadowed by '@property gettext' on the same class — pylint marks the import unused.

naming-shadowing.title: redefined-outer-name when importing subpackage of package which is already imported in outer scope
naming-shadowing.note: Confirmed W0621 false positive: 'def foo(): import os.path' when 'import os' is at module scope. (Plus W0611 unused-import on outer 'import os' which is a separate concern.)

arguments.title: False Negative ``no-value-for-parameter`` for instances used inside of a class
arguments.note: Confirmed: E1120 'no-value-for-parameter' is fired for direct 'seagull.flap()' but NOT for 'bird.flap()' or 'self.bird.flap()' inside BirdTester even with typed Bird annotation. False negative.

arguments.title: Missing mandatory keyword argument (E1125) with explicit mandatory keywords and keyword dictionary
arguments.note: Confirmed E1125: passing **someargs that contains 'c' and 'd' to fun(*, c, d, **kwargs) still raises missing-kwoa. Checker assumes literal kwargs only.

arguments.title: False positive `unbalanced-dict-unpacking`
arguments.note: Confirmed W0644 unbalanced-dict-unpacking false positive: even after 'if len(self.data) == 1:' narrowing the unpack of a single (k,v) tuple still warns.

arguments.title: False positive invalid-sequence-index using properties of range object as index
arguments.note: Confirmed E1126: l[r.start], l[r.stop] (range object's int properties) trigger invalid-sequence-index. astroid doesn't expose range's start/stop/step as int.

arguments.title: false-positive: no-value-for-parameter
arguments.note: Confirmed E1120 false positive: 'self.__new__(type(self))' raises no-value-for-parameter (the bug is pylint treats __new__ as needing 'cls' even though self.__new__ passes it implicitly... wait — actually __new__ is a staticmethod and self.__new__ DOES need cls. Reporter is wrong about the bug being a FP). Confirmed still raises E1120 either way.

arguments.title: invalid-sequence-index when unpacking a sequence of sequences
arguments.note: Confirmed E1126 false positive: 'for a1,a2,a3,a4 in n:' where n has 4+ p1 entries and p1 is a 4-tuple. Tuple unpacking in for-loop loses element type info.

arguments.title: False positives for E1120 and E1123 when instance method is overwritten
False negative ``repeated-keyword`` for builtin functions
arguments.note: Confirmed false negative persists: 'print(end=" !", **{"end": ":("})' — the duplicate 'end' keyword is not flagged repeated-keyword for builtin functions.

arguments.title: arguments-differ: number of parameters was some number ... and is now the same number in overridden ...
arguments.note: Confirmed W0221 message wording bug: 'Number of parameters was 2 in ... and is now 2 in overriding ...' — the count is the same; the real issue (kw-only conversion) isn't communicated.

arguments.title: option to treat `TYPE_CHECKING` as `True`, or add a separate variable
arguments.note: Confirmed E1120: 'if TYPE_CHECKING: def __init__(self): ... else: def __init__(self, value): ...' — pylint uses the TYPE_CHECKING branch for the call check, ignoring the runtime else-branch.

arguments.title: incorrect line number and missing module name for E0633 (and false-positive)
arguments.note: Confirmed E0633 false positive AND misleading message: 'a, b = NewType_wrapped_tuple' raises 'unpacking-non-sequence defined at line 10 of <empty module name>'.

arguments.title: `repeated-keyword` false-positive when overriding existing dict keys
arguments.note: Confirmed E1132 false positive: 'dict(kwargs, that=3)' merges dict (overriding), passed as 'my_func(**new_kwargs)' — pylint sees both 'that' values in original kwargs and override.

arguments.title: False positive unpacking-non-sequence on classmethod + property
arguments.note: Confirmed E0633 false positive: @classmethod @property returning tuple, unpacked as 'x, y = E.p'. astroid doesn't track tuple type through chained classmethod+property.

arguments.title: False-positive no-value-for-parameter for some unpacked values of list-builtins
arguments.note: Confirmed E1120 false positive: 'list(returns_implicit_tuple(1))' loses the implicit-tuple's element count; 'foo(*args)' flagged no-value-for-parameter. The non-list-wrapped variant works.

arguments.title: disable=unexpected-keyword-arg doesn't work when placed on line where keyword is specified
arguments.note: Confirmed: 'c=3 # pylint: disable=unexpected-keyword-arg' on the kwarg line doesn't suppress E1123 (raised on the open-paren line). Plus I0021 useless-suppression false positive.

arguments.title: False positive no-value-for-parameter in metaclass
arguments.note: Confirmed E1120: classmethod-like calls via metaclass instance ('Dang.baz()' → 'cls.bar("woo")') flagged 'No value for argument string in unbound method call'.

arguments.title: False positive for ``redundant-keyword-arg`` in class with ``partial()`` class attribute function
arguments.note: Confirmed E1124 redundant-keyword-arg false positive: 'partial(foo, "")' assigned as class attribute, called via 'self.bar(y=y)' — pylint sees 'y' as both positional (from partial) and keyword.

walrus.title: Conflicting warnings R2004 from `pylint.extensions.magic_value` and R6103 from `pylint.extensions.code_style`
walrus.note: Confirmed: applying R6103's walrus suggestion in the magic_value example still leaves R2004. Either R6103 should not apply here, or R2004 should look through walrus. Real interaction bug.

walrus.title: `used-before-assignment` when using `walrus operator(:=)` in dict, generator, some comprehensionswith function
walrus.note: Confirmed E0601 used-before-assignment false positive: walrus operator inside dict-literal argument to print()/dict() not understood; bare dict-literal works fine.

walrus.title: False positive `used-before-assignment` with walrus operator inside binary operation
walrus.note: Confirmed E0601 false positive: walrus operator inside binary 'd' + (... if (s1 := 'x') else '') — only flagged when the f-string is on either side of +; bare conditional works.

walrus.title: False positive 'undefined-variable' with assignment expression in decorator
walrus.note: Confirmed E0602: walrus 'foo := preprocess(string)' inside list comprehension that is itself inside a decorator argument; the inner 'foo' references after walrus are flagged undefined.

design-metrics.title: False positive ``too-few-public-methods`` on property function calls and descriptors
design-metrics.note: Real false positive R0903 'too-few-public-methods' for the property-equivalent forms via class-attribute property() / __get__ descriptors. Verified by labels and author's diff.

design-metrics.title: [too-many-function-args] False negative when calling `super().__init__()` with non-self argument
design-metrics.note: Confirmed false negative: super().__init__(txt) in a class where the MRO ultimately resolves to object.__init__() (takes no args) is not flagged too-many-function-args.

other.title: W1514 unspecified-encoding false positive when mode is specified using a variable and it can be 'b' (binary mode)
other.note: W1514 unspecified-encoding fires when mode is a variable that could be binary. Real false positive — checker doesn't handle parameterized mode strings.

other.title: E1142 false & missed alarms
other.note: Confirmed E1142: false alarm on line with 'await x' inside a generator-expression returning awaits, AND missing alarm on '[x async for x in agen()]' inside a sync function. Mixed-case awaitable/async-comprehension scope detection bug.

other.title: False positive `declare-non-slot` on classvar
other.note: Confirmed E0245 declare-non-slot false positive: 'x: ClassVar[int]' (no default) is treated as slot violation, while 'y: ClassVar[int] = 2' and 'z = 3' are not.

other.title: False positive `deprecated-class` when the class is imported in a guarded import block
other.note: Confirmed: deprecated-class W4904 fires inside 'if sys.version_info > (2,):' guarded import block. Mirrors #10043.

other.title: `not-callable` false positive for class
other.note: Confirmed E1102 not-callable false positive: TYPE_CHECKING-guarded @property declaring -> Type[X] (class reference) called as func.myfunc(1,2,3) flagged not-callable.

refactor-suggestions.title: findings not reported until seemingly unrelated code is changed
refactor-suggestions.note: Confirmed false negative: f() body's unnecessary-negation is not reported, blocked by an upstream inference involving subprocess.Popen.communicate. Reporter notes the missing finding is broader than C0117.

refactor-suggestions.title: False negative ``superfluous-parens`` when around two conditional and an ``and``
refactor-suggestions.note: Confirmed false negative C0325 (superfluous-parens) on 'if (a and b):' patterns. Other comparison checkers fire (R1727, R0124, R0133), but superfluous-parens does not.

refactor-suggestions.title: consider-using-augmented-assign false-positive for multiple dictionaries and string keys
refactor-suggestions.note: Confirmed R6104 consider-using-augmented-assign false positive on 'accumulated_values[key] = accumulated_values[previous] + values[key]' where LHS dict-key and RHS first-term dict-key differ.

refactor-suggestions.title: False positive: useless-parent-delegation on '__init__' method of class derived from 'Exception'
refactor-suggestions.note: Confirmed W0246 useless-parent-delegation false positive on Exception subclass __init__ that calls super().__init__() — Exception's __init__ accepts varargs so a no-arg override IS a behavior change.

refactor-suggestions.title: C0325 (superfluous-parens) appears to not trigger when contents are a string
refactor-suggestions.note: Confirmed false negative C0325: 'if x == ("option2"):' and 'if x in ("option4"):' do NOT trigger superfluous-parens (single-string in parens). Only kw-then-paren form triggers.

refactor-suggestions.title: False positive unnecessary-negation for float comparison
refactor-suggestions.note: Confirmed C0117 unnecessary-negation false positive: for floats, 'not a < b' is NOT equivalent to 'b <= a' under NaN. Checker doesn't consider float semantics.

refactor-suggestions.title: useless-parent-delegation false positive when __init__ signatures differ but parent is built-in type
refactor-suggestions.note: Confirmed W0246 useless-parent-delegation false positive: child of list/dict that takes no args in __init__ while parent takes optional args is flagged useless.

refactor-suggestions.title: `unnecessary-comprehension` false positive
refactor-suggestions.note: Confirmed R1721 unnecessary-comprehension false positive on '[(x, y) for x, y in a]' (tuple-repack from list-of-lists).

refactor-suggestions.title: False positive `C2801` `unnecessary-dunder-call` for descriptor binding `__get__`
refactor-suggestions.note: Confirmed C2801: method_ref.__get__(instance, class_ref) triggers unnecessary-dunder-call though descriptor binding has no public method.

refactor-suggestions.title: Unnecessary use of a comprehension: Wrong fix
refactor-suggestions.note: Confirmed: 'unnecessary-comprehension' suggests 'use dict(dict1) instead' when dict1's keys are tuples — would create a copy, not the intended dict-from-keys behavior. Wrong-fix bug.

refactor-suggestions.title: consider-using-f-string is triggered in a functional context
refactor-suggestions.note: Confirmed C0209 consider-using-f-string false positive on 'map("{}".format, …)' — the format method is used in functional context, can't easily be rewritten as f-string.

refactor-suggestions.title: False negative `trailing-comma-tuple`
refactor-suggestions.note: RE-VERIFIED: R1707 trailing-comma-tuple FN persists — pylint doesn't flag 'DEMO = True; print("demo"),' even though it does flag the same line without DEMO=True (control-flow context).

refactor-suggestions.title: False positive ``expression-not-assigned`` when calling functions with no return inside ternary expression
refactor-suggestions.note: Confirmed W0106 expression-not-assigned still fires on 'f1() if test else f2()'. Reporter argues this is the canonical conditional-call pattern.

refactor-suggestions.title: False Positive `dict-init-mutate` when dict assignment references dict
refactor-suggestions.note: Confirmed C3401 dict-init-mutate false positive: 'counts[key] = ... counts.get(key, 0)' references the dict itself, cannot be moved into the initializer.

refactor-suggestions.title: consider-using-generator / R1728 false positive for "AsyncGenerator" valid code
refactor-suggestions.note: Confirmed R1728 false positive: consider-using-generator suggests rewriting 'tuple([_ async for _ in my_gen()])' → 'tuple(_ async for _ in my_gen())' which raises TypeError.

refactor-suggestions.title: False positive for `implicit-str-concat` when some but not all strings are raw
refactor-suggestions.note: Confirmed W1404 false positive: r-string + plain string concat (intentional, to escape raw treatment for part of string) is flagged implicit-str-concat.

refactor-suggestions.title: False positive for `try-except-raise` with diamond inheritance
refactor-suggestions.note: Confirmed W0706 try-except-raise false positive on diamond-inherited exception: 'except ArithmeticError: raise' is a behavior difference from removing the handler.

refactor-suggestions.title: False-positive unneeded-not when comparing dict views
refactor-suggestions.note: Confirmed C0117 unneeded-not false positive: 'not {}.items() <= {}.items()' rewriting to '>' changes meaning for set-like dict views.

refactor-suggestions.title: Unexpected suppression of line-too-long messages
refactor-suggestions.note: Confirmed I0021 useless-suppression false positive: '# pylint: disable=line-too-long' on a short line raises useless-suppression even when intentional.

refactor-suggestions.title: Incorrect W0611 : Unused import when preceded by import as
refactor-suggestions.note: RE-VERIFIED: W0611 unused-import FN persists — 'import os.path as path' + later 'path' usage as attribute access. The R0402 consider-using-from-import suggestion fires instead (a style hint, not the unused-import detection).

unused.title: False positive `unused-import` when using union of types inside quotes
unused.note: Confirmed W0611: 'List[int] | None' inside a quoted forward annotation is not parsed as a usage, so 'from typing import List' is flagged unused.

abstract-classes.title: W0223 does not get raised correctly
abstract-classes.note: Confirmed false negative W0223: 'abstract-method' is not raised on Derived(Base) when Base inherits 'abc.ABC' directly. Raising it only when class uses metaclass=ABCMeta explicitly.

abstract-classes.title: Subclasses of abstract class that do not inherit abc.ABC are considered abstract
abstract-classes.note: RE-VERIFIED: W0223 abstract-method FN persists — Sub(AbsSub) where AbsSub inherits abc.ABC indirectly does not warn. The class hierarchy makes pylint think Sub is still abstract.

astroid-inference.title: AddressFamily and SocketKind are not visible in module socket
astroid-inference.note: Confirmed E0611: 'from socket import AddressFamily, SocketKind' is flagged even though they're real socket attributes (defined dynamically by socket module). astroid stub gap.

astroid-inference.title: False-positive E1137 (unsupported-assignment-operation) with np.empty_like
astroid-inference.note: Per reporter: 'from numpy import empty_like' results in E1137 unsupported-assignment-operation while 'np.empty_like([1])' (qualified) does not. Lib-specific astroid inference asymmetry.

astroid-inference.title: `not-callable` false positive for `types.FunctionType`
astroid-inference.note: Confirmed E1102 not-callable false positive on 'types.FunctionType(code, {})()'. astroid doesn't recognize FunctionType instances as callable.

pyreverse.title: ``pyreverse`` should check that ``klass`` is still ``ClassDef``
pyreverse.note: Pyreverse failure mode: 'klass.root().name' when klass is Uninferable raises TypeError. Code-reference identified; needs guard.

exceptions.title: False Positive - ``assignment-from-none`` raised when function not necessarily returns `None`
exceptions.note: Confirmed E1128 false positive: get_func() conditionally returns 'def func(): return None' OR 'def func(): return param' — pylint sees only the None branch and flags 'val = func()' as assignment-from-none.

exceptions.title: Initializer arguments not checked when raising exception with implicit instantiation.
exceptions.note: Confirmed: 'raise Bang' (without instantiation) still doesn't check __init__ args. False negative for implicit exception instantiation.

pragma.title: Pylint disable working incorrectly
pragma.note: Confirmed bug: # pylint: disable-next=C0301 on a too-long comment line doesn't disable just that comment; needs --enable to recover. Pragma scope issue.

match-case.title: False positive with match/case for `function-redefined`
match-case.note: Confirmed E0102 function-redefined false positive: 'def function():' in match/case 'case "c":' branch flagged as redefinition of earlier 'function = function_a' assignment in 'case "a":' branch.

docs.title: Docparams does not raise `differing-type-doc` for Sphinx documentation
docs.note: Confirmed FN persists: differing-type-doc not raised when Sphinx-format docstring mismatches type hint.

config.title: False positive ``attribute-defined-outside-init`` when using properties
config.note: RE-VERIFIED: W0201 attribute-defined-outside-init STILL fires on property(get,set) where set assigns self._value. Initial FIXED determination was wrong because pylint's own pylintrc disables attribute-defined-outside-init.
